scenario: Forge Manufacturer dispatches 5000 shipments per day in Camunda 8.8. After a parallel split, three branches run in parallel: pack, label, and invoice. A parallel join gateway downstream must wait for every branch to arrive before continuing to the dispatch task.
question: How does a parallel join gateway in Camunda 8 decide when to forward a token to its outgoing flow?

options.text: The join consumes one token from every incoming sequence flow and then produces a single token on the outgoing sequence flow
The join produces a single outgoing token as soon as the first incoming token arrives and discards later tokens to avoid duplication
The join forwards every incoming token to the outgoing flow with no synchronization, producing a token per branch downstream
The join requires an explicit FEEL completion condition expression on the gateway to decide when enough branches have arrived

a.text: Correct. A BPMN parallel gateway joins by consuming exactly one token from each incoming sequence flow and producing one token on the outgoing flow.

b.text: Incorrect. The parallel gateway synchronizes; it does not forward on first arrival and never silently discards branch tokens.

c.text: Incorrect. Forwarding every token without synchronization describes an inclusive merge without join semantics, not a parallel join.

d.text: Incorrect. Parallel joins do not use completion conditions; that pattern belongs to multi-instance activities, not gateways.

explanation: The parallel gateway joins by consuming one token from every incoming flow and emitting one token on its outgoing flow, synchronizing parallel branches.
